name: OpenText Core Content Management API
what_it_does: OpenText Core Content (cloud ECM) REST API for enterprise content management, document storage, records management, and digital workspace collaboration. Enables AI agents to manage document creation, upload, and versioning for automated enterprise content lifecycle management, handle folder and workspace management for project-based content organization, access records management and retention scheduling for regulatory content governance, retrieve full-text search and metadata filtering for enterprise content discovery automation, manage content workflows and approval routing for business process automation, handle digital asset classification and tagging for content intelligence automation, access security and access control management for content governance, retrieve audit trail and activity logging for compliance reporting, manage content migration and bulk import for ECM consolidation workflow, and integrate OpenText Core Content with Microsoft 365, SAP, Salesforce, and enterprise applications via REST and OpenText OCP platform.
use_cases: Managing enterprise documents from content lifecycle agents
Enforcing records retention from compliance automation agents
Routing approvals from business process agents
Classifying content from AI tagging agents
not_for: Lightweight team file sharing (use SharePoint or Google Drive)
Construction document control (use Procore or Oracle Aconex)
best_when: An enterprise organization wanting AI agents to automate document lifecycle management, records retention enforcement, content workflow routing, and governance across regulated industries within the OpenText Core Content cloud ECM platform.
avoid_when: NARA FEDERAL RECORDS MANAGEMENT COMPLIANCE: Automated records management via OpenText Core Content in US federal agency context must comply with NARA regulations (44 USC Chapter 31, 36 CFR Chapter XII) including retention schedule accuracy and disposition authorization; automated record disposition without NARA-compliant authorization creates federal records violation. GDPR RIGHT TO ERASURE FOR EU CONTENT: Automated content retention enforcement for EU personal data must support GDPR Article 17 right to erasure; automated retention hold that blocks erasure of EU personal data creates GDPR compliance conflict with legal hold management; implement legal hold vs erasure conflict resolution workflow. 21 CFR PART 11 FOR REGULATED LIFE SCIENCES DOCUMENTS: Automated electronic records and signatures in OpenText Core Content for FDA-regulated documents must comply with 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and electronic signature requirements; automated document management without validated 21 CFR Part 11 configuration creates FDA regulatory risk. OPENTEXT ACQUISITION COMPLEXITY: OpenText acquired Micro Focus (including several ECM products) in 2023; Core Content is one of multiple OpenText ECM products including Documentum, eDOCS, and Content Suite; verify product consolidation roadmap and Core Content investment continuity before integration.

auth.notes: OpenText Core Content uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes via OpenText Cloud Platform (OCP). REST API with JSON. Waterloo, Ontario HQ (OpenText Corporation, NASDAQ: OTEX). OpenText $6B+ annual revenue post-Micro Focus acquisition. Core Content is OpenText's cloud-native modern ECM (SaaS), differentiated from legacy OpenText Content Suite (on-premise) and Documentum (also acquired). OpenText OCP platform provides unified OAuth and API gateway. Competes with Microsoft SharePoint, Box, and M-Files for cloud enterprise content management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-user annual subscription with storage tiers
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per user with storage capacity tiers; enterprise volume pricing available
pricing.notes: Waterloo ON. OpenText NASDAQ:OTEX. $6B+ revenue. Core Content is cloud-native SaaS ECM. Micro Focus acquired 2023. Per-user annual subscription with storage tiers.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; bulk upload and full-text search throttled; webhooks for content events

known_agent_gotchas: OPENTEXT ECM PRODUCT PORTFOLIO CONFUSION: OpenText has multiple ECM products — Core Content (cloud SaaS), Content Suite (on-premise), Documentum, and eDOCS; verify which OpenText ECM product a customer uses before building integration; APIs differ significantly between products and are not interchangeable

GDPR RIGHT TO ERASURE VS LEGAL HOLD CONFLICT: Automated records retention enforcement must handle conflict between GDPR Article 17 erasure requests and legal hold (litigation hold) on same document; automated erasure of held records creates legal hold violation; implement legal hold status check before automated erasure or retention enforcement

21 CFR PART 11 VALIDATION FOR LIFE SCIENCES: Automated OpenText Core Content use in FDA-regulated life sciences requires GxP validation against 21 CFR Part 11 requirements (audit trail, electronic signatures, access control); automated document management without validated configuration creates FDA inspection finding

OCP OAuth token and tenant isolation — OpenText Cloud Platform (OCP) OAuth issues tenant-scoped tokens; automated agent tokens are tenant-specific and cannot cross OpenText tenant boundaries; multi-tenant deployments require separate OAuth client per tenant with independent token management

Full-text search relevance and metadata filtering — automated OpenText Core Content search for document retrieval must use metadata filters alongside full-text search; metadata-only search on large enterprise repositories returns excessive results; combine classification metadata (document type, department) with full-text query for precise retrieval

Bulk upload and migration throttling — automated bulk document import to OpenText Core Content triggers upload throttling for large file sets; implement chunked upload with rate-limit-aware batching; monitor throttle headers and implement backoff for large content migration workflows
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise cloud ECM. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP. OAuth2/OCP. US/EU/UK/AU. Enterprise document and records data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise ECM. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP. OAuth2/OCP. Global. Enterprise document and records data.
